title: alstroemeria andina subsp. andina
intro: Alstroemeria andina subsp. andina, also known as the high-altitude Peruvian lily or Inca flower, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Alstroemeria genus. It is native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes in South America and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
